images.caption: Figure 1:Comparison between Jet-Long and baseline methods, applied on Qwen3-1.7B-Base, on per-length accuracy aggregated over all 13 RULER tasks and per-length perplexity in PG-19, alongside single-batch generation throughput on H100 at 128K context (the worst length we test). Jet-Long achieves the highest accuracy and lowest perplexity at extended context lengths, preserves the base model’s pretrained performance within the training context, and incurs≤4%\leq 4\%latency overhead relative to FlashAttention-2(dao2023flashattention2).

Figure 3:RULER accuracy as a function of context length, averaged over the 13 RULER tasks, on Qwen3-1.7B/4B/8B-Base. Jet-Long preserves the pretrained model’s performance within the training window (32K) and outperforms YaRN and DNTK at all extended context lengths (the full comparison vs. DCA and Self-Extend is in Table1).
